description: Runtime policy for the local Rippling-style Deep Agents fixture (supervisor + read/RAG/action subagents). Analog of the Devin PR-gate policy, but for a tool-calling runtime agent instead of a diff-producing coding agent.

bonus_sanity.description: Not passed to Policy.from_dict (Policy.check_output()'s numeric_ranges/output_schema apply globally to every tool's output, which does not fit a multi-tool-shape fixture like this one -- a read-agent record has no bonus_amount field). Instead this bound is checked tool-locally inside issue_payroll_bonus() in rippling_fixture_agent.py: an amount over the cap is still recorded (allowed), but flagged in the tool's own output -- pinned into the tamper-evident receipt for forensics. This is the 'plausible in-range lie' framing from examples/POISONED_MCP_DEMO.md Act 3: the tool can lie about a sane amount, but it cannot lie to the receipt.

governor.description: Sidecar block consumed directly by rippling_fixture_agent.py to build RuleBasedSandboxGovernor(). Not parsed by Policy.from_dict.

protected_invariants.message: documented invariant, not enforced by a runtime check in this pass -- agentauth/receipts/sandbox_governor.py has no call-ordering primitive, and bolting one on as a fixture-local hack risked an uncaught exception path in ReceiptedMcpGateway.call_tool(). The commit-token requirement below is the control this pass actually enforces for writes; adding real call-ordering enforcement is future work on RuleBasedSandboxGovernor itself, not this example.
every write must carry a signed commit token bound to the exact tool name, arguments, query_id, and authority epoch

poison_markers.description: Keyword/regex markers used only by the narrated demo (demo/rippling_deepagents_demo.py) to call out injected instructions in RAG docs/skills/records for the audience. Not used for JSONL pass/fail assertions -- the deterministic harness only asserts on the downstream tool-call outcome, since a text-scanning check is not part of the core policy engine.
